title:
Lesson 1:
I have, You have
description: 'Tener' (to have) is crucial. 'Tengo' = I have, 'Tienes' = you have. Also used for age in Spanish (I have 5 years = I am 5 years old).

title:
Lesson 2:
Colors
description: Basic colors with gender agreement. 'Rojo/roja' (red), 'azul' (blue), 'verde' (green). Adjectives must match the gender of the noun.

title:
Lesson 3:
I like
description: 'Me gusta' = I like (literally 'it pleases me'). This verb works differently - 'gusta' for singular, 'gustan' for plural. Very common expression!

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I like it a lot.

title:
Lesson 4:
Common Actions
description: Present tense regular -ar verbs. 'Hablar' (to speak): 'hablo' = I speak, 'hablas' = you speak. 'Estudiar' (to study): 'estudio', 'estudias'.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Yes, I speak English and Spanish.

title:
Lesson 5:
Working
description: 'Trabajar' (to work): 'trabajo' = I work, 'trabajas' = you work. Common -ar verb for daily life.

title:
Lesson 6:
To read
description: 'Leer' (to read): 'leo' = I read, 'lees' = you read. Regular -er verb. Talk about reading books, magazines, etc.

title:
Lesson 7:
To write
description: 'Escribir' (to write): 'escribo' = I write, 'escribes' = you write. Regular -ir verb.

title:
Lesson 8:
The weather
description: Weather expressions: 'Hace calor' = It's hot, 'Hace frío' = It's cold, 'Llueve' = It rains, 'Nieva' = It snows.

title:
Lesson 9:
Clothing
description: Clothing vocabulary: camisa (shirt), pantalones (pants), zapatos (shoes), vestido (dress). 'Llevar' = to wear.

title:
Lesson 10:
Body parts
description: Basic body parts: cabeza (head), ojos (eyes), manos (hands), pies (feet). 'Me duele' = It hurts me.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: No estoy bien. Me duele la cabeza.
dialogue.english: I'm not well. My head hurts.

title:
Lesson 11:
The house
description: House vocabulary: casa (house), habitación (room), cocina (kitchen), baño (bathroom), sala (living room).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: El baño está a la derecha.
dialogue.english: The bathroom is to the right.

title:
Lesson 12:
Animals
description: Common animals: perro (dog), gato (cat), pájaro (bird), pez (fish), caballo (horse).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Yes, I like them a lot.

title:
Lesson 13:
At school
description: School vocabulary: escuela (school), clase (class), libro (book), lápiz (pencil), maestro/maestra (teacher).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Mi libro está en la clase.
dialogue.english: My book is in the class.

title:
Lesson 14:
To need
description: 'Necesitar' (to need): 'necesito' = I need, 'necesitas' = you need. Very useful verb for expressing needs.

title:
Lesson 15:
To want
description: 'Querer' (to want): 'quiero' = I want, 'quieres' = you want. Irregular verb. Very common!

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you want to eat?

title:
Lesson 16:
To go
description: 'Ir' (to go): 'voy' = I go, 'vas' = you go. Very irregular but essential verb!

title:
Lesson 17:
To do/make
description: 'Hacer' (to do/make): 'hago' = I do/make, 'haces' = you do/make. Irregular but very common!

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: What do you do on weekends?

title:
Lesson 18:
To see/watch
description: 'Ver' (to see/watch): 'veo' = I see, 'ves' = you see. Used for watching TV, movies, etc.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Sí, veo televisión todos los días.
dialogue.english: Yes, I watch television every day.

title:
Lesson 19:
There is/are
description: 'Hay' means both 'there is' and 'there are'. Very useful for describing what exists or is available.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Sí, hay un banco en la calle.
dialogue.english: Yes, there is a bank on the street.

title:
Lesson 20:
Question words review
description: Key question words: ¿Qué? (what), ¿Quién? (who), ¿Dónde? (where), ¿Cuándo? (when), ¿Por qué? (why), ¿Cómo? (how), ¿Cuánto? (how much).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: She's at home. When are you going?

title:
Lesson 21:
Negation
description: To make a sentence negative, put 'no' before the verb. 'No hablo' = I don't speak. Simple and consistent!

title:
Lesson 22:
My, your, his, her
description: Possessive adjectives: mi/mis (my), tu/tus (your), su/sus (his/her/their). Plural forms add -s.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Sus libros están en la mesa.
dialogue.english: His/Her books are on the table.

title:
Lesson 23:
Ser vs Estar review
description: 'Ser' for identity, characteristics, origin, time. 'Estar' for location, states, feelings, progressive actions.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Sí, y estoy muy contento aquí.
dialogue.english: Yes, and I am very happy here.

title:
Lesson 24:
To open/close
description: 'Abrir' (to open): 'abro' = I open. 'Cerrar' (to close): 'cierro' = I close. Note: cerrar is irregular (e→ie).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What time do they open the store?

title:
Lesson 25:
Hobbies
description: Hobbies vocabulary: nadar (swim), correr (run), bailar (dance), cantar (sing), pintar (paint), cocinar (cook).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you like to do?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I like to swim. And you?

title:
Lesson 26:
To play (sports)
description: 'Jugar' (to play): 'juego' = I play, 'juegas' = you play. Irregular (u→ue). Use 'jugar a' + sport.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I play soccer on Saturdays.

title:
Lesson 27:
To listen
description: 'Escuchar' (to listen): 'escucho' = I listen, 'escuchas' = you listen. Regular -ar verb.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What music do you listen to?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you listen to music every day?

title:
Lesson 28:
To understand
description: 'Entender' (to understand): 'entiendo' = I understand, 'entiendes' = you understand. Irregular (e→ie).

title:
Lesson 29:
To know (facts)
description: 'Saber' (to know facts/how to): 'sé' = I know, 'sabes' = you know. Irregular first person.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you know where the bank is?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you know how to speak English?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I know how to speak English.

title:
Lesson 30:
To know (people/places)
description: 'Conocer' (to know people/be familiar with): 'conozco' = I know, 'conoces' = you know. Different from 'saber'!

title:
Lesson 31:
To be able/can
description: 'Poder' (can/to be able): 'puedo' = I can, 'puedes' = you can. Irregular (o→ue). Very useful!

title:
Lesson 32:
Frequency adverbs
description: Frequency words: siempre (always), nunca (never), a veces (sometimes), generalmente (generally), todos los días (every day).

title:
Lesson 33:
Intensifiers
description: Intensifiers: muy (very), mucho (a lot/much), poco (little/few), bastante (quite/enough), demasiado (too much).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Me too. I work a lot.

title:
Lesson 34:
Conjunctions
description: Common conjunctions: y (and), pero (but), o (or), porque (because), cuando (when).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Me gusta el café pero no el té.
dialogue.english: I like coffee but not tea.

title:
Lesson 35:
Adjective agreement
description: Adjectives must match noun in gender and number: libro nuevo (m), casa nueva (f), libros nuevos (m pl), casas nuevas (f pl).

title:
Lesson 36:
Comparisons
description: Comparisons: más... que (more... than), menos... que (less... than), tan... como (as... as).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: El tren es más rápido que el autobús.
dialogue.english: The train is faster than the bus.

title:
Lesson 37:
This, that, these, those
description: Demonstratives: este/esta/estos/estas (this/these near), ese/esa/esos/esas (that/those medium), aquel/aquella (that far).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, but I prefer that book.

title:
Lesson 38:
Near future
description: 'Ir a' + infinitive = going to do something. 'Voy a comer' = I'm going to eat. Easy way to talk about future!

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What are you going to do tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Are you going to eat with us?

title:
Lesson 39:
Making plans
description: Using near future to make plans: 'vamos a' (we're going to), invitations, accepting/declining.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Voy a ir al cine. ¿Quieres venir?
dialogue.english: I'm going to the movies. Do you want to come?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: We're going to go on Saturday.

title:
Lesson 40:
At the doctor
description: Medical vocabulary: enfermo/enferma (sick), dolor (pain), medicina (medicine), doctor/doctora, hospital.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: You should go to the doctor.

title:
Lesson 41:
Feeling words
description: Emotions with estar: cansado (tired), feliz (happy), triste (sad), enojado (angry), nervioso (nervous).

title:
Lesson 42:
Asking permission
description: '¿Puedo...?' = Can I...? / May I...? Polite way to ask for permission.

title:
Lesson 43:
Telephone phrases
description: Phone vocabulary: llamar (to call), teléfono (phone), mensaje (message), hablar (to speak).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: No, she's not here. Who's speaking?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you want to leave a message?

title:
Lesson 44:
Describing things
description: Adjectives for things: grande (big), pequeño (small), nuevo (new), viejo (old), bonito (pretty), feo (ugly).

title:
Lesson 45:
Seasonal expressions
description: Seasons: primavera (spring), verano (summer), otoño (fall), invierno (winter). Weather in each season.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: A mí me gusta el verano.

title:
Lesson 46:
Daily routine
description: Routine verbs: levantarse (get up), ducharse (shower), desayunar (have breakfast), acostarse (go to bed).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What time do you get up?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Yo me levanto a las seis.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you shower in the morning?

title:
Lesson 47:
Expressing preferences
description: 'Preferir' (to prefer): 'prefiero' = I prefer. Irregular (e→ie). Use with nouns or infinitives.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you prefer coffee or tea?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you prefer to read or watch television?

title:
Lesson 48:
Asking for opinions
description: Opinion phrases: '¿Qué piensas?' (What do you think?), '¿Qué opinas?' (What's your opinion?), 'Creo que...' (I think that...).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you think of this movie?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What's your opinion of the restaurant?

title:
Lesson 49:
Giving suggestions
description: 'Deber' (should): 'debes' = you should. 'Deberías' = you should (conditional). Giving advice.

title:
Lesson 50:
Expressing agreement
description: Agreement: 'Estoy de acuerdo' (I agree), 'Tienes razón' (You're right), 'Exacto' (Exactly), 'Claro' (Of course).

title:
Lesson 51:
Expressing disagreement
description: Disagreement: 'No estoy de acuerdo' (I don't agree), 'No creo' (I don't think so), 'Al contrario' (On the contrary).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Sí, al contrario, es muy emocionante.
dialogue.english: Yes, on the contrary, it's very exciting.

title:
Lesson 52:
Talking about abilities
description: 'Saber' + infinitive = to know how to do something. Talking about skills and abilities.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you know how to cook?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I know how to cook well.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you know how to play an instrument?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: No, no sé tocar ningún instrumento.
dialogue.english: No, I don't know how to play any instrument.

title:
Lesson 53:
Talking about experience
description: Using 'ya' (already), 'todavía no' (not yet), 'nunca' (never) to talk about experiences.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: No, I have never visited Spain.

title:
Lesson 54:
Introducing the past
description: Simple past (preterite) of -ar verbs: hablé (I spoke), hablaste (you spoke). Regular endings: -é, -aste, -ó.

title:
Lesson 55:
Past tense -er/-ir
description: Preterite of -er/-ir verbs: comí (I ate), comiste (you ate), vivió (he/she lived). Endings: -í, -iste, -ió.

title:
Lesson 56:
Yesterday activities
description: Using past tense with 'ayer' (yesterday), 'anoche' (last night), 'la semana pasada' (last week).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Last night I studied a lot.

title:
Lesson 57:
Past tense: ir
description: 'Ir' in past tense: fui (I went), fuiste (you went), fue (he/she went). Irregular but common!

title:
Lesson 58:
Past tense: hacer
description: 'Hacer' in past: hice (I did), hiciste (you did), hizo (he/she did). Irregular spelling change.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What did you do on the weekend?

title:
Lesson 59:
Past tense: tener
description: 'Tener' in past: tuve (I had), tuviste (you had), tuvo (he/she had). Irregular.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you have a good day?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I had an excellent day.

title:
Lesson 60:
Past tense: estar
description: 'Estar' in past: estuve (I was), estuviste (you were), estuvo (he/she was). Irregular.

title:
Lesson 61:
Talking about the past
description: Combining different past tense verbs to describe what happened. Building narrative skills.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Yesterday I went to the movies.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Yes, I liked it a lot.

title:
Lesson 62:
Reflexive verbs intro
description: Reflexive verbs: me lavo (I wash myself), te lavas (you wash yourself). Action done to oneself.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What time do you wake up?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: ¿Te lavas la cara por la mañana?
dialogue.english: Do you wash your face in the morning?

title:
Lesson 63:
Direct object pronouns
description: Direct object pronouns: me (me), te (you), lo/la (him/her/it), nos (us), los/las (them). Replace nouns.

title:
Lesson 64:
Indirect object pronouns
description: Indirect object pronouns: me (to me), te (to you), le (to him/her), nos (to us). Show who receives action.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you give the book to Juan?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I gave him the book.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Will you lend me your pencil?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I'll lend you my pencil.

title:
Lesson 65:
Weather past tense
description: Talking about weather in the past: 'Hizo calor' (It was hot), 'Llovió' (It rained), 'Nevó' (It snowed).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What was the weather like yesterday?

title:
Lesson 66:
Making invitations
description: Inviting someone: '¿Quieres...?' (Do you want to...?), '¿Te gustaría...?' (Would you like to...?).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you want to go to the concert?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I would like to go.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: When do you want to go?

title:
Lesson 67:
Accepting invitations
description: Accepting: '¡Claro!' (Sure!), '¡Por supuesto!' (Of course!), 'Me encantaría' (I would love to), '¡Perfecto!' (Perfect!).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you want to come to my house?

title:
Lesson 68:
Declining invitations
description: Declining politely: 'Lo siento' (I'm sorry), 'No puedo' (I can't), 'Tal vez otro día' (Maybe another day).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you want to go to the movies tonight?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Tengo que estudiar. Tal vez otro día.
dialogue.english: I have to study. Maybe another day.

title:
Lesson 69:
Expressing necessity
description: 'Tener que' + infinitive = to have to do something. 'Necesitar' = to need. Expressing obligations.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you have to do today?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I need to finish a project.

title:
Lesson 70:
Sequencing events
description: Sequence words: primero (first), después (then/after), luego (later), finalmente (finally), por último (lastly).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: First I went to the gym.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Then I went to the supermarket.

title:
Lesson 71:
Talking about duration
description: Duration: 'desde' (since), 'hace' (for/ago), 'durante' (during). How long something has been happening.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: How long have you been studying Spanish?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Estudio español desde hace un año.
dialogue.english: I've been studying Spanish for a year.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Yo estudio desde hace seis meses.
dialogue.english: I've been studying for six months.

title:
Lesson 72:
Expressing doubt
description: Expressing uncertainty: 'No estoy seguro' (I'm not sure), 'Tal vez' (Maybe), 'Quizás' (Perhaps), 'Probablemente' (Probably).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Is it going to rain tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Are you going to the party?

title:
Lesson 73:
Review and celebration
description: Congratulations on reaching lesson 100! Review key concepts: greetings, verbs, time, daily life, past tense. Keep practicing!

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: We did it! We finished one hundred lessons.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, now I speak more Spanish.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: It's true. I am very happy.
